definitions: The act of committing a person to an institution, especially a mental health institution or prison.
The act of committing a body to a grave or a crematorium.
The act of committing oneself to a course of action or belief; a pledge or promise.
The act of referring a bill or other matter to a committee for consideration.
examples: The judge ordered his committal to a psychiatric hospital.
The committal of the deceased took place at noon.
Her committal to the project was unwavering.
The committal of the new legislation to committee was delayed.
